learning_objective: Students execute a systematic fact-checking workflow when presented with an environmental claim, reaching an evidence-based conclusion.
specification:
Type: workflow **sim-id:** fact-check-workflow
**Library:** p5.js
**Status:** Specified **Bloom Level:** Apply **Bloom Verb:** Execute **Learning Objective:** Students execute a systematic fact-checking workflow when presented with an environmental claim, reaching an evidence-based conclusion. **Instructional Rationale:** An interactive decision-tree format makes the abstract process concrete and gives students a repeatable tool they can use beyond the classroom. **Visual Elements:** - Flowchart/decision tree starting with "Environmental Claim" at the top - Branching paths based on SIFT steps: Stop → Investigate Source → Find Coverage → Trace to Original - At each decision node, yes/no questions guide the user - Terminal nodes: "Likely reliable," "Needs more investigation," "Likely misleading," "Likely false" - Confidence meter showing how strength of conclusion changes at each step - Sample claims panel on the left for practice **Interactions:** - Select a sample claim from a library (10+ claims of varying reliability) - Walk through the decision tree, answering questions at each node - At "Investigate Source," click to see source details (funding, credentials, history) - At "Find Coverage," see what different sources report about the claim - At "Trace to Original," read a simplified version of the original source - Final assessment compared to expert evaluation with explanation of any differences - "Bring your own claim" text input for free-form practice **Colors:** Reliable: green (#2a9d8f), uncertain: yellow (#e9c46a), misleading: orange (#f4a261), false: red (#e63946), workflow path: navy (#264653), active node: bright blue (#457b9d)
